Class OpenALAudioManager

java.lang.Object
net.fokinatorr.sparkle.audio.impl.OpenALAudioManager
All Implemented Interfaces:
AutoCloseable, AudioManager, NonThrowingAutoCloseable
public class OpenALAudioManager extends Object implements AudioManager
The entry point for creating an OpenAL audio engine.

The audio manager is not thread-safe. All methods except close() should be called from the same thread that calls tick() (usually the main/game loop).

Note: Do not manually close sound source objects returned by an instance of this class. Instead, use the destroySoundInstance(String) method for proper cleanup.

    Creates a new audio manager that opens the default audio device.

  • Constructor Details

    • OpenALAudioManager

      public OpenALAudioManager(AppContext appContext)
      Creates a new audio manager that opens the default audio device.
      Parameters:
      appContext - the application context (used to get sound decoders)
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if initialization fails for any reason

  • Method Details

    • loadSound

      public void loadSound(Path path) throws IOException
      Loads a sound from file at the given path. If a sound is already loaded at the given path, it is closed, removed and replaced with a new one. The sprite/stage assets are loaded automatically, so there's usually no need to call this method manually.

      See SoundDecoder for supported formats list.

      Specified by:
      loadSound in interface AudioManager
      Parameters:
      path - the path to the sound file
      Throws:
      IOException - if something goes wrong during loading, or if the file format isn't recognized
      See Also:
      • AudioManager.loadSounds(Path...)
      • AudioManager.loadSounds(Iterable)
      • SoundDecoder

    • hasLoadedSound

      public boolean hasLoadedSound(Path path)
      Determines whether a sound is already loaded from file at the given path. This can be useful to prevent unnecessary sound reloading. This is always true for sprite/stage assets since they're loaded automatically.
      Specified by:
      hasLoadedSound in interface AudioManager
      Parameters:
      path - the file path
      Returns:
      true if a sound from file at the given path is already loaded by this manager, false otherwise.

    • createStaticSoundInstance

      public it.unimi.dsi.fastutil.Pair<String, StaticSoundSource> createStaticSoundInstance(Path path)
      Creates a new StaticSoundSource from the loaded sound at the given path. A sound must be loaded with loadSound(Path) before calling this method.
      Specified by:
      createStaticSoundInstance in interface AudioManager
      Parameters:
      path - the path to the loaded sound
      Returns:
      a pair containing the ID of the new sound source and the sound source itself
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if no sound is loaded at the given path
      IllegalStateException - if the audio manager is closed

    • createStreamingSoundInstance

      public it.unimi.dsi.fastutil.Pair<String, StreamingSoundSource> createStreamingSoundInstance(Path path, boolean loop)
      Creates a new "streaming" sound instance from the loaded sound at the given path.

      Streaming sound instances are dynamic; they don't store their data all at once but instead request chunks on demand. This works well with long ambient music tracks, since they don't get loaded entirely immediately, which would cause lags.

      A sound must be loaded at the given path using AudioManager.loadSound(Path) before calling this method. (For sprite/stage assets, this is done automatically.)

      Note for Scratchers: This is different from how sounds work in Scratch. In Scratch, all sounds are loaded fully before playing. Here, streaming allows very long sounds (like background music) to play without delay and with low memory usage. Use this for tracks longer than ~30 seconds.

      Specified by:
      createStreamingSoundInstance in interface AudioManager
      Parameters:
      path - the path to the (already loaded!) sound file
      loop - whether the sound instance must loop
      Returns:
      a Pair containing:
      • Key: The unique ID of the new sound instance
      • Value: The sound instance itself, ready to play
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if no sound is loaded at the given path
      IllegalStateException - if this audio manager is closed

    • createStreamingSoundInstance

      public it.unimi.dsi.fastutil.Pair<String, StreamingSoundSource> createStreamingSoundInstance(Path path, int maxBufferSize, boolean loop)
      Creates a new streaming sound source from the loaded sound at the given path. A sound must be loaded with loadSound(Path) before calling this method.
      Parameters:
      path - the path to the loaded sound
      maxBufferSize - the maximum size (in samples per channel) for each streamed buffer
      loop - whether the source should loop
      Returns:
      a pair containing the ID of the new sound source and the sound source itself
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if no sound is loaded at the given path
      IllegalStateException - if the audio manager is closed

    • getSoundInstance

      public BaseSoundSource<?> getSoundInstance(String id)
      Returns a sound source by the given ID, if any, otherwise returns null.
      Specified by:
      getSoundInstance in interface AudioManager
      Parameters:
      id - the ID
      Returns:
      the respective sound source or null
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the audio manager is closed

    • destroySoundInstance

      public void destroySoundInstance(String id)
      If a sound source with the given ID exists, stops, closes and removes it, otherwise does nothing.
      Specified by:
      destroySoundInstance in interface AudioManager
      Parameters:
      id - the ID
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the audio manager is closed

    • tick

      public void tick()
      Advances the audio manager by one tick. Should be called regularly from the main/game loop.
      Specified by:
      tick in interface AudioManager
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the audio manager is closed

    • close

      public void close()
      Destroys this audio manager. The manager can not be used after call to this method. Calling this method more than once does not have any effect.
      Specified by:
      close in interface AudioManager
      Specified by:
      close in interface AutoCloseable
      Specified by:
      close in interface NonThrowingAutoCloseable
